text:
## Complete Eye Infection Detection Application Built

I've created a complete full-stack web application for preliminary eye infection detection. Here's what was generated:

### Project Structure

```
EYE/
├── frontend/
│   ├── index.html          # Mobile-first responsive UI
│   ├── style.css           # Clean, modern styling
│   └── script.js           # Frontend logic & API calls
├── backend/
│   ├── main.py              # FastAPI REST API
│   ├── test_api.py          # API testing utility
│   ├── .env.example         # Configuration template
│   ├── model/               # Directory for trained models
│   └── utils/
│       ├── preprocessing.py # Image preprocessing
│       └── inference.py     # Model inference wrapper
├── training/
│   └── eye_detection_training.ipynb  # Google Colab notebook
├── requirements.txt        # Python dependencies
├── setup.sh               # Automated setup script
├── .gitignore             # Git ignore rules
├── README.md              # Complete documentation
└── QUICKSTART.md          # Quick start guide
```

### Key Features Implemented

**Frontend:**
- Mobile-first responsive design (Android compatible)
- Image upload with drag & drop support
- Image preview functionality
- Real-time AI prediction display
- Clear results with confidence scores
- Medical disclaimer prominently displayed

**Backend (FastAPI):**
- POST /predict endpoint for image analysis
- Automatic image preprocessing (resize 224x224, normalize)
- Model inference with trained CNN
- JSON responses with predicted_class and confidence_score
- CORS enabled for frontend integration
- Health check endpoint

**Machine Learning:**
- Transfer learning using MobileNetV2
- TensorFlow/Keras implementation
- 4 classes: Normal, Conjunctivitis, Cataract, Glaucoma
- Data augmentation for robustness
- Two-phase training (frozen + fine-tuning)
- TFLite export for mobile optimization

**Training Notebook:**
- Complete Google Colab notebook
- Dataset loading and preparation
- Model architecture and training
- Evaluation metrics (accuracy, AUC, confusion matrix)
- Visualizations (training curves, predictions)
- Model saving in .keras, .h5, and .tflite formats
- Inference speed testing

### Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Setup
bash setup.sh

# 2. Train model in Google Colab
# Open training/eye_detection_training.ipynb
# Run all cells, download model files
# Place in backend/model/

# 3. Start backend
source venv/bin/activate
cd backend && python main.py

# 4. Open frontend
cd frontend && python -m http.server 8001
# Visit http://localhost:8001
```

### Scalability Features

- Modular architecture for easy expansion
- Easy to add new eye conditions
- PWA-ready for mobile app conversion
- Docker-ready deployment
- RESTful API for integration
- TFLite export for native mobile apps

All files include clear comments and explanations. The LSP warnings shown are type-checking issues that won't affect runtime execution.
